title: oxytropis lanata
intro: Oxytropis lanata, also known as the lamb's tail or woolly locoweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the pea family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in glycosides, flavonoids, and other compounds that have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Oxytropis lanata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusions, and is often used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory conditions.
